name: Kh.Kim
summary: I’m Kihwan Kim, a Frontend Engineer. At RIDI, I worked on performance optimization, cross-device stability, and server-driven UI migration for a large-scale content platform. I’m currently at Levvels, a subsidiary of Dunamu, building AI-powered browser agents, generative UI, and commerce frontend experiences. During my master’s studies, I researched visual analytics and AI-driven recommender interfaces, and I bring that analytical mindset into product engineering, interaction design, and frontend architecture.

experience.summary: Working across AI product and creator commerce, with a focus on browser agents, generative UI, and commerce frontend.

projects.title: PageAgent QA/CS Automation
projects.summary: Extended Alibaba PageAgent into an internal browser agent for QA/CS automation.
details: Automated repetitive QA/CS flows
Explored Chrome-profile-based deployment
Structured operations for real workplace use

projects.title: Generative UI and AI Chat Interfaces
projects.summary: Explored ways to render LLM responses as structured UI.
details: Connected LLM responses to UI components
Improved streaming and scroll stability
Designed choices, branching, and regeneration flows

projects.title: Creator Commerce Frontend
projects.summary: Continuously operate and improve commerce frontend flows for Vuddy and creator channels.
details: Maintain and improve cart and authentication systems
Manage operations flows with Amplitude and ChannelTalk
Organize reusable frontend patterns through the design system

experience.summary: Introduced RiGrid server-driven UI for RIDI’s 2.13M MAU content platform, enabling operator-led UI changes and faster experiments.

projects.title: RIDI Web Platform
projects.summary: Contributed to RiGrid and island-architecture migration.
details: Rendered server-declared UI on the client
Migrated PHP pages toward React
Made A/B tests and operational UI changes more flexible
Kept mobile, desktop, and special-device stability

projects.title: Large-scale List Rendering Optimization
projects.summary: Improved rendering bottlenecks and perceived speed for 6,000+ item lists.
details: Analyzed frame drops from large DOM trees
Rendered only the visible range with windowing
Handled scroll position and dynamic heights
Improved skeleton UI for perceived speed

projects.title: User Environment Stabilization
projects.summary: Improved real-user stability with Sentry tracking and browser/device fixes.
details: Tracked real-user errors with Sentry
Handled translation-plugin conflicts
Improved E-ink rendering and UX issues

experience.summary: Worked on AutoML product planning and interfaces that translated AI capabilities into usable service experiences.

projects.title: Legacy System React Migration
projects.summary: Ported enterprise platform products from a jQuery-based internal library to React.
details: Modularized legacy UI code and improved interface structure
Developed reusable components to replace bespoke legacy widgets

projects.title: AutoML Platform
projects.summary: Designed feature flows and frontend interactions for an AutoML platform.
details: Implemented a codeless studio for non-expert model builders
Developed explainable AI (XAI) visualizations and dashboards
Researched interfaces between AutoML engines and model developers
Clarified requirements for an early-stage platform

education.institution: Ulsan National Institute of Science and Technology (UNIST)

degrees.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
Bachelor of Science in Technology Management (Multi-disciplinary major in Computer Science)

projects.title: AI-driven UI and Recommender System Research
projects.description: Studied how exploratory items should be disclosed in recommender-system interfaces to collect better user logs and feedback.
points: Measured the impact of transparency on data quality in the explore-exploit problem of recommendation systems
Analyzed how disclosure patterns for exploratory items affect user perception and data quality
Proposed a metric to evaluate the value of user logs from an AI perspective
Implemented a web-based movie recommendation system for experimental environments
Designed data-driven UI experiments and evaluation flows

projects.title: Modeling Web User Behavior
projects.description: Conducted research to understand and model user behavior patterns in web environments.
points: Estimated user reward functions from behavior logs through Inverse Reinforcement Learning
Dealt with various web environments such as data analysis tools like Tableau, history education tools based on Wikipedia documents, and card games
Utilized data analysis tools such as Tableau and implemented a history education tool based on Wikipedia documents

publications.title: An Empirical Analysis on Transparent Algorithmic Exploration in Recommender Systems
points: Investigated how to deliver random items to capture user preferences in recommendation systems
Implemented a web-based movie recommendation system used as an experimental environment
Proposed a metric to evaluate the value of user logs from an AI perspective
Measured the impact of transparency on data quality in the explore-exploit problem of recommendation systems
Recruited 94 participants from Amazon MTurk
Collected real usage log data and survey responses by having participants use a Netflix-like experimental environment

publications.title: ST-GRAT: A Novel Spatio-temporal Graph Attention Networks for Accurately Forecasting Dynamically Changing Road Speed
publications.conference: ACM International Conference on Information and Knowledge Management (CIKM), 2020
points: Preprocessed vehicle detection sensor data installed on the road surface by Korea Expressway Corporation
Categorized cases where attention worked effectively by patterns

publications.title: Modeling Exploration/Exploitation Decisions through Mobile Sensing for Understanding Mechanisms of Addiction
publications.conference: ACM International Conference on Mobile Systems, Applications, and Services (MobiSys), 2019
points: Proposed a system to detect addiction disorders from smartphone usage logs through Inverse Reinforcement Learning

publications.title: An Empirical Study on the Relationship Between the Number of Coordinated Views and Visual Analysis
points: Conducted experiments to see how the number of visualization charts affects data visual analysis
Had 44 participants use a visual analysis tool and solve data analysis tasks
Categorized users' analysis patterns through think-aloud protocol, recorded screens, and log data
Observed a positive correlation between the number of charts and task scores

publications.title: A Survey of Visualization Techniques for Interpreting Deep Learning
points: Summarized and investigated visualization techniques for interpreting deep learning models
